channel.title: Posts on Reion's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Reion's Blog

item.title: Redesigning My Blog, GOV.UK Style
item.description: I recently redesigned this blog. The visual direction came from GOV.UK Design System. I checked alphagov/govuk-frontend for implementation details, then asked codex-cli with gpt-5.4 to do most of the coding.

item.title: A Finder Resume Copy Issue
item.description:
I hit a weird Finder copy failure the other day.

This was on macOS Sequoia. The copy failed halfway, and Finder said I could resume it. I didn’t really want to trust that.

item.title: Mesa EGL Notes
item.description: Mesa sits in the middle of most Linux graphics stacks. On a modern desktop it is usually the layer that turns EGL calls into driver operations, talks to DRM/KMS, and connects rendering APIs to the native window system.

item.title: Android Photo Export Pitfall
item.description:
adb pull can mess up file timestamps when exporting photos from Android if you forget -a.

adb pull -a /storage/emulated/0/DCIM ./DCIM

That flag is easy to miss. It saves a lot of cleanup later.

item.title: How KDE Plasma Badge Counts Work
item.description: Today I logged into Telegram on KDE Plasma and noticed something hard to ignore: the badge count on the taskbar icon updates really nicely. It’s a small detail, but also very visible, because badge counts are meant to pull your attention back to unread activity instead of quietly sitting there.

item.title: Emacs Dired Mimeicon mode
item.description: This extension displays file and folder icons in dired mode. It uses mimetype as the displayed logo and follows the icon-theme-spec file naming convention, so you can use icon themes downloaded from the internet in your emacs.

item.title: A Notification Design Idea
item.description: I recently saw the new front-screen design on the iPhone 14 pro series, which Apple calls “Dynamic island”, and that’s where I got the idea.

item.title: Cutefish Menu Bar Chameleon
item.description: macOS Big Sur introduced a new top menu bar behavior: the menu bar background color adapts to the desktop wallpaper, so it feels more integrated than before.

item.title: C++ PIMPL Pattern
item.description: PIMPL means pointer to implementation, or private implementation. It shows up a lot in C++ projects and libraries. The basic idea is simple: keep private data and methods out of the public interface. Put the class implementation details behind a separate pointer-owned class. This is useful when building a stable ABI for a C++ library interface, and for cutting down compile-time dependencies.
